name: Armor & AC
summary: Armor sets your AC — the number an attack must beat before you even Parry.

sections.heading: AC comes first
paragraphs: Armor Class (AC) is the number an attacker must meet or beat on its attack roll to hit you. Only a hit can be Parried, so armor helps by keeping attacks from reaching the Parry step at all. AC and Parry are two layers of defense, not the same one.

sections.heading: Armor categories
list: Light: base AC + your full Dexterity modifier.
Heavy: a fixed base AC, no Dexterity; some require a minimum Strength or cost you speed.
Shield: +2 AC on top of whatever armor you wear.

sections.heading: Armor does not affect Parry
paragraphs: Wearing heavy armor does not make you worse at Parrying. Some classes parry by bracing and absorbing rather than dodging, so armor is never a penalty to it. How good you are at Parrying is set by your class's Parry DC, not by what you wear.
